name: Dave Tough
abstract: David Jarvis Tough (April 26, 1907 – December 9, 1948) was an American jazz drummer associated with  Dixieland and swing jazz in the 1930s and 1940s. He has been described as "the most important of the drummers of the Chicago circle".

works.t: Love is Just Around the Corner
I Don't Stand a Ghost of a Chance With You

about_quotes.speaker: John LaPorta
about_quotes.evidence: the band that I was on, 1944 to ‘46 with Dave Tough and Flip Phillips and Bill Harris and people like that.
about_quotes.doc_title: John LaPorta interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 13, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: And then a little later on we got Davie Tough (Bull) on drums from Park Highschool. And another guy by the name of Dave North on piano, so that was our band.

about_quotes.speaker: Art Hodes
about_quotes.evidence: I hired Eddie Condon, Mezz Crow, Cliff Jackson, Frankie Newton and Dave Tough at seven dollars apiece, on Collins Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Chubby Jackson
about_quotes.evidence: he taught me how to play bass. And he would capture my enthusiasm and put it in place.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chubby Jackson interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 14, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Flip Phillips
about_quotes.evidence: His bandmates included Chubby Jackson, Dave Tough and Bill Harris.
about_quotes.doc_title: Flip Phillips interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 11, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Bud Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: I just moved right up on them as though I was one of them and I put my hand out to shake hands with the great Dave Tough, and he treated me as though I were one of the musicians. It was marvelous to me. And we became --
